name: Does a lung infection from the office AC count as a work injury?

mainEntity.name: Does a lung infection from the office AC count as a work injury?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if it is a serious condition like Legionnaires\' disease or a fungal infection caused by workplace contaminants. Success depends on proving the building\'s environment was the source, often through air quality tests and medical records linking the specific pathogen to the site.
acceptedAnswer.description: Standard "walking pneumonia" or a common cold usually doesn't qualify, but serious infections like Legionnaires\' disease or fungal pneumonia caused by office AC systems or mold are covered. You must prove that the workplace was the specific source of the pathogen, which is often done through environmental testing of the building\'s water or ventilation systems. If other coworkers also got sick, your case becomes much stronger. The insurance carrier will likely argue you caught the virus in the community, so immediate testing of the workplace is vital. You should document the dates you noticed smells, leaks, or poor air quality. Legal intervention is often required to force an employer to release internal air quality reports.
